scenario: A local newsroom receives a synthetic example image with a checked credential, then tracks what happens as the image is resized for a CMS, reposted to a social platform, screenshotted into a group chat, and reused in a classroom slide.

original_item.description: Synthetic storm-shelter photo supplied directly by a staff photographer for media-literacy practice.
original_item.source_or_holder: Newsroom asset folder; synthetic file only; no real person or event depicted.
original_item.credential_check: Credential inspected in the newsroom asset tool before export; signer and timestamp recorded in local notes, not reproduced here.

chain_steps.transformation: No export yet; first trusted copy retained.
chain_steps.review_note: Credential supports chain-of-custody for the file copy, not whether the caption is complete or whether the scene represents a broader trend.

chain_steps.transformation: Image resized and compressed for article layout.
chain_steps.what_broke_or_changed: Credential indicator remains visible in the asset system but export metadata is reduced.
chain_steps.review_note: Keep the original asset link and caption notes with the story package.

chain_steps.transformation: Article image preview is copied into a platform card.
chain_steps.what_broke_or_changed: Platform preview does not show the newsroom credential state to ordinary viewers.
chain_steps.review_note: Viewer should follow back to the publisher page and asset note rather than relying on the preview.

chain_steps.transformation: A reader screenshots the social post and shares it in a private chat.
chain_steps.what_broke_or_changed: Screenshot has no usable file provenance from the original image.
chain_steps.review_note: Treat screenshot as a pointer to investigate, not as a verified copy.

chain_steps.transformation: Teacher pastes the screenshot beside a discussion question.
chain_steps.what_broke_or_changed: Students see a static image without original credential context unless the teacher adds the source-chain note.
chain_steps.review_note: Slide should include the publisher link, original credential note, and explicit caveat that provenance is not truth.

alternate_corroboration: Compare the classroom screenshot against the publisher page and original synthetic asset note.
Check whether the caption, date, and location were preserved across each reuse.
Ask a human editor or teacher to decide whether the slide needs a separate context note before use.
do_not_claim: Do not claim the credential proves the depicted event happened as captioned.
Do not claim the screenshot is fake just because credentials were stripped.
Do not use this synthetic example as evidence about real storms, shelters, platforms, or credential tool performance.
next_review_step: Attach this receipt to the media-literacy lesson and keep the original synthetic asset separate from screenshots or exported copies.
boundary_notes: Synthetic example only; no live political, emergency, medical, legal, or real-person claim is evaluated.
Local review aid only; no network call, account action, public posting, or forensic claim is made.
Credential survival is only one trust signal; context and source corroboration still require human review.
